hook: Brand new vs resale landed: the data shows who blinked first

slides.sub: Brand new vs resale — where deals are closing, at what price, and who's cutting first.

slides.lead: Resale landed caveats keep lodging — but the PSF tells the real story.

slides.pre: Sentosa Cove, Coral Island — Feb 2025 print
slides.post: vs S$20,082 psf peak (Jul 2021) — 99-yr lease, 737 sqm. Price discovery, not collapse.

slides.title: Three things the Coral Island print tells us

items.body: S$14.8M closed Feb 2025 (737 sqm) — same unit last traded at S$17.4M in Feb 2023, a 15% nominal cut.
Sentosa Cove 99-yr leasehold is past its halfway mark; lease decay is now a live pricing variable.
Buyers are still transacting — but only at a discount wide enough to offset tenure risk and ABSD.

slides.title: Resale landed PSF by cluster, latest caveats
slides.source: URA caveats · POV Guy analysis (psf = price ÷ land area)

slides.label: Brand new vs resale
slides.punch: Brand new landed commands a 15–25% premium for clean title and zero renovation risk. Resale sellers know this, which is why asking prices are sticky — but the caveats show the real clearing level is lower. The discount exists; you just have to know where to find it.
slides.punch_bold: The gap between ask and transacted is where your deal lives.

slides.title: Where resale landed volume is holding up

items.body: Faber Hills (D5 OCR) logged 6 caveats in 2024 alone — semi-Ds ranging S$6.88M–S$8.39M, showing sustained buyer demand.
Springwood (D5 RCR, freehold terraces) traded S$3.9M–S$6.2M across 2025, with the latest Mar 2026 print at S$5.58M.
Sentosa Cove volume has thinned to 1–2 trades per year — price discovery phase, not a liquid market.

slides.pre: Eyeing landed in D4, D5 or D10?
slides.title: Let's map your
slides.sub: 30-min strategy call. No pitch — just the caveats, the comps, and where the real discount sits.

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: Know anyone weighing brand new vs resale landed right now? Send them this before they sign anything.

caption:
📉 Resale landed is still moving — but caveats show sellers are blinking.

• Coral Island (D4, 99-yr) cleared at S$14.8M in Feb 2025 — 15% below its Feb 2023 trade
• Faber Hills semi-Ds (D5 OCR, freehold) logged 6 caveats in 2024: S$6.88M–S$8.39M range
• Springwood terraces (D5 RCR, freehold) latest print Mar 2026: S$5.58M — steady, not surging

Brand new commands a premium. Resale is negotiable. The deal is in the gap between ask and the last caveat.
